text.title: vui-privacy-security Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for vui-privacy-security as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Embedding real user PII or API secrets in the system prompt, treating it as private.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The system prompt is semi-public — adversaries extract it via creative jailbreaks. Any secrets or PII embedded in prompts will be leaked eventually.

failure-mode.description: Assuming "delete from our database" removes voice data from all persistence layers.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Cloud transcripts persist on third-party infrastructure (OpenAI, Google, AWS). Local deletion does not guarantee removal from provider storage unless Zero Data Retention is explicitly negotiated and documented in the DPIA.

failure-mode.description: Trusting voice speaker ID as sufficient authentication for high-value actions.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Voice cloning is accessible and speaker ID fails under noise. Adversaries can bypass voice biometrics with clones; step-up with a knowledge or possession factor is mandatory for high-risk actions.

failure-mode.description: Logging tool-call payloads without redacting PII at the boundary.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Tool calls typically log request and response payloads in full. PII redaction must happen at the tool boundary before any persistence layer receives the data — not as a post-processing step.
